CONTENT.Club career: Fontaine made his professional debut on 30 July 2012, coming on as a substitute for Yacoub Meite in the 4–0 defeat to Monaco.On 29 January 2022, Fontaine signed with Nancy.After a brief spell with Beroe in Bulgaria, Fontaine joined TFF First League club Gençlerbirliği on a one-and-a-half-year deal on 10 January 2023.On 21 August 2023, Fontaine joined Championnat National club Sochaux on a two-year deal.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of 30 June 2019As of matches played on 20 November 2023Scores and results list Madagascar's goal tally first.
CONTENT.International career: Fontaine represented the France U21 national team at the 2011 FIFA U-20 World Cup. Fontaine made his senior debut for Madagascar in a 2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification win over Sudan on 9 June 2017. He represented the national team at 2019 Africa Cup of Nations.

SUMMARY: Thomas Fontaine (born 8 May 1991) is a professional footballer who plays as a defender for Championnat National club Sochaux. Born in the French region of Réunion, Fontaine represents the Madagascar national team internationally.
